title: Selection and insertion sort

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Selection and insertion sort; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Selection and insertion sort
sources.claim: Insertion, selection, and bubble sort expose different invariants, stability, adaptiveness, and quadratic movement or comparison behavior.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Insertion, selection, and bubble sort expose different invariants, stability, adaptiveness, and quadratic movement or comparison behavior.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Insertion sort keeps a sorted prefix and inserts the next value into its correct prefix position. Your goal is to implement, trace and compare selection and insertion sort on representative inputs. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Arrays, strings and matrices; Time, space and asymptotic growth; Array representation and operations. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Insertion, selection, and bubble sort expose different invariants, stability, adaptiveness, and quadratic movement or comparison behavior. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, pass buffers and counts and use qsort/bsearch comparators carefully; in C++, prefer standard algorithms and strict comparators; in Java, use Arrays/Collections algorithms with comparator contracts; in Python, use sorted/list.sort and bisect with key and stability semantics.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Small or nearly sorted inputs can favor simple algorithms, but asymptotic and write costs still differ. The draft therefore rejects: All quadratic sorting algorithms perform exactly the same comparisons and preserve equal-item order.

includes: Use elementary sorts to practice invariants, stability and quadratic growth.
Implement, trace and compare selection and insertion sort on representative inputs.

In C, pass buffers and counts and use qsort/bsearch comparators carefully; in C++, prefer standard algorithms and strict comparators; in Java, use Arrays/Collections algorithms with comparator contracts; in Python, use sorted/list.sort and bisect with key and stability semantics.
excludes: Small or nearly sorted inputs can favor simple algorithms, but asymptotic and write costs still differ.

outcomes: Implement, trace and compare selection and insertion sort on representative inputs.
